task: I need to leave a handoff note about a room issue for the next shift. It should include the important details clearly so nothing gets missed when they read it later.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a clear handoff note template you can use for a room issue:

**Handoff Note: Room Issue**
- **Room:** [room number/name]
- **Issue:** [brief description of the problem]
- **When noticed:** [time/date]
- **Current status:** [what has been done so far]
- **Impact:** [guest impact / safety / service impact]
- **Actions taken:** [repairs requested, guest informed, maintenance called, etc.]
- **Pending follow-up:** [what the next shift needs to do]
- **Priority:** [low / medium / high]

**Example:**
**Handoff Note: Room Issue**
- **Room:** 214
- **Issue:** AC not cooling properly
- **When noticed:** 9:15 PM on 4/29
- **Current status:** Guest reported room is warm
- **Impact:** Comfort issue for guest
- **Actions taken:** Maintenance ticket submitted; fan delivered to room
- **Pending follow-up:** Next shift should confirm maintenance response and check guest satisfaction
- **Priority:** High

If you want, I can also turn this into a more polished **hotel-style handoff note** or a **shorter one-line version**.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, but I need the note to be about an actual room problem, not just a template. Make it plain and specific. Something like:

Room 214 — AC not cooling right. Guest said the room felt warm around 9:15 PM. Maintenance was called and a fan was brought up. Please check first thing next shift to make sure it’s fixed and see if the guest needs anything else.

If you want, I can give you the real note format I’d write for housekeeping, short and clean.

turns.agent_response: Room 214 — AC not cooling properly. Guest reported the room felt warm around 9:15 PM. Maintenance was called and a fan was delivered to the room. Please check first thing next shift to confirm it’s fixed and see if the guest needs anything else.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Plain and clear. I’d leave it like that.
